deep_dive.title: Stable Transformer-Actor-Critic Model Predictive Control: A Contraction Analysis Approach
deep_dive.abstract: Actor-Critic Model Predictive Control (MPC) effectively addresses complex, non-convex control problems, but guaranteeing the closed-loop stability of sequence-based learning models within these pipelines remains challenging. This paper introduces a novel Transformer-Actor-Critic MPC architecture with formal robustness guarantees. First, we prove that Transformer networks can satisfy global incremental Input-to-State Stability ($δ$ISS). We then leverage Riemannian contraction theory to analyze the interconnected dynamics between the physical plant and the predictive neural network. Finally, we integrate these theoretical bounds as a training regularizer to yield a certifiably robust policy. The framework is validated on a nonlinear 3D drone model executing target-reaching and obstacle-avoidance maneuvers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: provides stable and robust control policy using Transformer-Actor-Critic MPC with contraction analysis, relevant to safe RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用变压器-演员-评论家MPC和收缩分析提供稳定鲁棒控制策略，与安全强化学习相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用变压器-演员-评论家MPC和收缩分析提供稳定鲁棒控制策略，与安全强化学习相关

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a Transformer-Actor-Critic MPC architecture with formal stability guarantees via incremental ISS and Riemannian contraction, validated on nonlinear 3D control.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对序列学习模型在模型预测控制中闭环稳定性难以保证的问题，本文提出一种变压器-演员-评论家MPC架构。证明了变压器网络满足增量输入-状态稳定性，利用黎曼收缩分析分析互联动态，并将理论界作为训练正则化器，得到可认证鲁棒策略。在非线性3D控制上验证了其有效性。

quick_skim.title: DNN Koopman-Based Deviation Compensation for UGV Path Tracking Control on Coupled Slope and Potholed Road
quick_skim.abstract: Unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs) operating in off-road scenarios are confronted with complex terrain disturbances that can substantially degrade path tracking performance. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a deep neural network (DNN) Koopman-based deviation compensation strategy for UGV path tracking control. Firstly, based on the vehicle dynamic function on coupled slope, an adaptive forgetting recursive least squares method with decoupled error terms is designed to estimate tire cornering stiffness. On this basis, a Laguerre model predictive control (LMPC) path tracking control strategy is designed by incorporating Laguerre functions, which can reduce computational resource usage while maintaining reliable tracking performance across different coupled slope scenarios. Then, by integrating Koopman operator theory with DNN, a DNN Koopman (DK) path deviation compensation method is proposed, which significantly improves the path tracking accuracy of UGV under potholed road disturbances. Furthermore, an event-triggered parallel cooperative (EPC) compensation mechanism that couples LMPC with DK is established based on compensation activation criteria and credibility verification. This mechanism improves path tracking accuracy on potholed road while ensuring the feasibility of overall steering command and stability of vehicle after DK compensation. Finally, a hardware-in-the-loop (HiL) experimental platform is constructed for validation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed UGV path tracking strategy improves tracking performance by more than 11.5% across multiple operating conditions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: utilizes deep neural network for UGV path tracking control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用深度神经网络进行无人车路径跟踪控制

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用深度神经网络进行无人车路径跟踪控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a DNN Koopman-based deviation compensation strategy for UGV path tracking control on rough terrain, integrating adaptive forgetting recursive least squares and Laguerre MPC for improved performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对无人地面车辆在复杂地形下的路径跟踪扰动问题，本文提出一种基于深度神经网络和Koopman理论的偏差补偿控制策略。该方法首先通过自适应遗忘递推最小二乘估计轮胎刚度，然后结合Laguerre模型预测控制减少计算负担。实验表明，在耦合坡度和坑洼路面上跟踪精度显著提升。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对无人地面车辆在复杂地形下的路径跟踪扰动问题，本文提出一种基于深度神经网络和Koopman理论的偏差补偿控制策略。该方法首先通过自适应遗忘递推最小二乘估计轮胎刚度，然后结合Laguerre模型预测控制减少计算负担。实验表明，在耦合坡度和坑洼路面上跟踪精度显著提升。

quick_skim.title: pdSTL: Probabilistic Differentiable Signal Temporal Logic for Stochastic Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Autonomous robots operating in uncertain environments must satisfy complex temporal and safety specifications despite stochastic dynamics and sensing noise. While Signal Temporal Logic (STL) offers robustness measures for gradient-based optimization, existing extensions either lack differentiability or ignore belief-space uncertainty. We introduce pdSTL (probabilistic differentiable Signal Temporal Logic), a framework that unifies probabilistic semantics with differentiable robustness over belief trajectories. pdSTL employs interval-valued probabilistic semantics to compute conservative satisfaction bounds, propagated compositionally through the STL syntax tree. We formulate the temporal robustness evaluation as a recurrent, LSTM-style unfolding of STL operators, enabling linear-time, differentiable monitoring suitable for end-to-end trajectory optimization. We validate pdSTL on simulated obstacle avoidance, lane-change maneuvers, and real-world Crazyflie quadcopter flight experiments under aerodynamic disturbances. Results demonstrate that pdSTL achieves efficient optimization with formal probabilistic guarantees, significantly outperforming deterministic differentiable STL in maintaining safety margins under real-world uncertainty.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: provides a probabilistic differentiable STL for safety specification in stochastic systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为随机系统提供概率可微信号时序逻辑用于安全规约
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为随机系统提供概率可微信号时序逻辑用于安全规约
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为随机系统提供概率可微信号时序逻辑用于安全规约
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces pdSTL, a framework that unifies probabilistic semantics with differentiable robustness over belief trajectories for safety-critical autonomous systems under uncertainty.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对随机动力学和感知噪声下的自动驾驶安全规约问题，本文提出pdSTL概率可微信号时序逻辑。该方法结合区间值概率语义和可微鲁棒性，以LSTM方式递归计算时序鲁棒性，实现梯度优化。实验表明，pdSTL可有效处理贝叶斯不确定性，为安全关键系统提供保守满足界。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对随机动力学和感知噪声下的自动驾驶安全规约问题，本文提出pdSTL概率可微信号时序逻辑。该方法结合区间值概率语义和可微鲁棒性，以LSTM方式递归计算时序鲁棒性，实现梯度优化。实验表明，pdSTL可有效处理贝叶斯不确定性，为安全关键系统提供保守满足界。

quick_skim.title: Safe Local Navigation for Ackermann-Steered Robots in Unmapped Environments
quick_skim.abstract: A control framework is proposed for safe local navigation of mobile robots equipped with Ackermann steering in unmapped environments where a global goal is absent. Based on local obstacle detections, the safest heading angle is determined along the direction of the largest open space ahead of the vehicle. Guided by this direction, bounding lines are constructed on the left and right sides of the vehicle to achieve obstacle separation. These bounding lines are obtained by solving a convex quadratic optimization that maximizes vehicle-to-obstacle clearance. Optionally, conditions are imposed on the bounding lines to preserve parallelism and smooth abrupt changes from prior control steps. A feedback-linearizing controller is then used to regulate the vehicle's distance from one or both bounding lines, effectively enabling tracking of a local reference path that preserves safety through obstacle clearance maximization. Open-source code is included for the application of this control scheme. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method produces safer navigation paths with significantly shorter computation times, compared to some existing exploration-based planners.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a safe local navigation control framework for Ackermann-steered robots in unmapped environments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出阿克曼转向机器人在未知环境中的安全局部导航控制框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出阿克曼转向机器人在未知环境中的安全局部导航控制框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出阿克曼转向机器人在未知环境中的安全局部导航控制框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a control framework for safe local navigation of Ackermann-steered robots in unmapped environments, using convex optimization for obstacle separation and feedback linearization for control.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 面向无全局目标未知环境中的阿克曼转向机器人，本文提出一种安全局部导航控制框架。该方法通过凸二次优化确定最大开放空间方向并构建边界线实现障碍分离，采用反馈线性化控制器进行跟踪。仿真和实验验证了该方法的安全性和有效性。
